author: y4rr
content: Restored fast_log_f32 in ops/elementwise.rs and wired it into vlog_f32 as the slow-path fallback for non-finite or non-positive inputs. This fixes two latent bugs in vlog_f32: +inf input returned ~88.7 (the polynomial's exponent path treats +inf's biased-255 exponent as a finite normal number) and NaN input returned arbitrary finite garbage (the bit-level mantissa extraction yields a normal float that the polynomial happily evaluates). Now both correctly produce +inf and NaN respectively. 7 new tests: 4 restored fast_log_f32 edge-case tests + 3 vlog_f32 regression tests pinning the IEEE-754 expected values. Gates: fmt clean, clippy --all-targets -D warnings clean, 1247 unit tests pass.
